notes: Earliest sequenced genomes collected 20 Sep 2020 in Kent, England. Phylogenetic analysis suggests emergence Jul-Aug 2020.
Earliest specimens from Eastern Cape Province (Nelson Mandela Bay). Phylogeographic analysis suggests emergence Jul-Aug 2020; earliest sequences ~Oct 8 2020 but some evidence of Aug 2020 samples.
Molecular clock estimates emergence ~21 Nov 2020 in Manaus (95% HPD: 12-29 Nov). Some evidence of ancestral lineage mid-Aug 2020. First international detection 6 Jan 2021 (Japan).
First detected in Maharashtra, India. PANGO earliest sequence 7 Dec 2020 but epidemiological reports cite 5 Oct 2020. Conservative cutoff uses Oct 2020.
First confirmed specimen collected 9 Nov 2021 in Botswana. Reported to WHO 24 Nov 2021. Designated VOC 26 Nov 2021.
First observed Jul 2020 in Los Angeles County (Cedars-Sinai). Molecular clock: MRCA ~20 May 2020. B.1.427 and B.1.429 diverged Jun-Jul 2020.
First detected by genome sequencing Oct 2020 in Rio de Janeiro. Estimated emergence ~early Jul 2020. Some sequences Apr-Aug 2020 may represent ancestral forms.
Earliest GISAID sequence 15 Dec 2020 from UK. First cases detected simultaneously in UK and Nigeria Dec 2020.
Study period begins 30 Oct 2020 but most confirmed Theta cases (94%) from first 2 weeks of Feb 2021. Likely emerged Jan 2021 in Central Visayas.
First sequenced late Nov 2020 in NYC (<1% of sequenced genomes). Phylogeographic analysis suggests ancestral viruses originating Aug 2020 in New York.
First detected in India. Some sources cite Oct 2020; PANGO earliest sequence 7 Dec 2020. Part of B.1.617 parent lineage first submitted fall 2020.
Earliest GISAID record from Argentina Nov 2020. First reported in Lima Dec 2020 (0.5% of genomes). Some evidence of circulation in Peru as early as Aug 2020.
Earliest collection date 11 Jan 2021 from Magdalena department, Colombia (accession EPI_ISL_1220045). Designated VOI Aug 2021.
Original dominant Omicron sublineage. Earliest specimens 9 Nov 2021 (same as parent B.1.1.529). Drove first Omicron wave globally.
Emerged early Nov 2021. Phylogenetic root estimated ~5 Nov 2021. First sequenced ~late Nov 2021 in South Africa. Became dominant globally by Mar 2022.
Designated alongside BA.1 and BA.2 but never became dominant. Earliest sequences mid-Nov 2021.
First detected from specimen collected 10 Jan 2022 in Limpopo, South Africa.
First detected from specimen collected 25 Feb 2022 in KwaZulu-Natal, South Africa. Became globally dominant by Jul 2022.
First detected in India ~May 2022. Also known as Centaurus. BA.2 sublineage with additional spike mutations.
First sequences reported to GISAID from Nigeria in early-mid Jul 2022. Sub-lineage of BA.5 (via BE.1.1.1). Carries additional K444T and N460K.
Recombinant of BA.2.75 (BM.1.1.1) and BJ.1 (BA.2.10.1). First identified in India Aug 2022. Formally noted 13 Sep 2022 from Singapore sequences on GISAID.
First identified in New York Oct 2022. Nicknamed Kraken. Carried additional F486P mutation enhancing ACE2 binding. Became dominant in US by Jan 2023.
Earliest sample collected 24 Jul 2023 in Denmark. Also early detections from Israel and USA same week. Nicknamed Pirola. >30 spike mutations vs BA.2.
Earliest sample collected 25 Aug 2023. Descendant of BA.2.86 with additional L455S spike mutation. Became globally dominant by Jan 2024.
